data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify uses a microservices architecture to manage different functionalities across its platform.
data.technical_spec: Each service is encapsulated, provides a specific function, and communicates via RESTful APIs or messaging queues.

data.title: Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Deployed on a scalable cloud infrastructure to handle global user demand.
data.technical_spec: Leverages services from cloud providers like Google Cloud for compute, storage, and networking solutions.

data.title: Data Driven System
data.description: Heavy reliance on data-driven decision-making to optimize user experience.
data.technical_spec: Integrates tools like Apache Kafka and Google BigQuery for data streaming and analysis.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Uses CDNs to ensure consistent music streaming quality and fast loading times around the world.
data.technical_spec: Partners with third-party CDN providers such as Akamai to cache content close to users.

data.title: Resilient and Redundant Systems
data.description: Focus on resilience with multiple redundancy layers to ensure high availability.
data.technical_spec: Implements failover mechanisms and disaster recovery plans.
